content.text:
<timestamp>Wednesday, Apr 29, 2026, 2:41 PM (UTC-4)</timestamp>
<user_query>
Make a plan for testing that we can use dark mode and light mode, and theming, that we can make sure it's accessible and everything works. Grill me and ask me questions to make sure we're 100% aligned on the work that we need to do.
</user_query>

Before diving into questions, I should examine the existing theme setup to understand what's already in place, then ask targeted questions to make sure we're aligned on the testing scope and priorities.

I've read enough to understand the theming setup. Before I draft a plan, I need to grill you on scope because there are a few surprises that completely change the plan.

**What I found:**
- Theme value (`light | dark | system`) is persisted in a signed `httpOnly` cookie via [src/server/user-session.server.ts](apps/org-next/src/server/user-session.server.ts) and read in [src/hooks/use-user-session.ts](apps/org-next/src/hooks/use-user-session.ts). 
- Theme tokens live in [packages/ui/src/styles/theme.css](packages/ui/src/styles/theme.css) with `:root` (light) and `.dark` (dark), and the Tailwind variant in [src/styles.css](apps/org-next/src/styles.css) is `@custom-variant dark (&:is(.dark *));`.
- A `ThemeProvider` (next-themes) and `ThemeToggle` exist in [packages/ui/src/components/theme-provider.tsx](packages/ui/src/components/theme-provider.tsx) and [packages/ui/src/components/theme-toggle.tsx](packages/ui/src/components/theme-toggle.tsx), **but org-next does not actually import or use them**. Grep finds no `ThemeProvider` / `useTheme` / `next-themes` usage in `apps/org-next`, and [src/routes/__root.tsx](apps/org-next/src/routes/__root.tsx) renders a plain `<html lang="en">` with no `class="dark"` toggling.
- There is no theme toggle UI wired into the app sidebar today, and no e2e/unit test currently covers theming.
- A hardcoded `a { color: oklch(42.4% 0.199 265.638); }` in [src/styles.css](apps/org-next/src/styles.css) doesn't change between modes — likely a contrast issue in dark mode.

In other words: **the cookie persists a theme value, but nothing in org-next actually applies the `.dark` class or honors `system`. So "dark mode" effectively does nothing today.**

That changes the question. Two critical clarifications before I plan:

 I'm mapping out how theming works in `org-next`: the theme preference gets stored server-side in a signed cookie that can be light, dark, or system, the actual theme tokens are defined as CSS custom properties in a shared styles file with separate selectors for light and dark modes, and there's a Tailwind variant that ties into this system.

But here's the issue—the `next-themes` provider that exists in the UI package isn't actually being used in `org-next`, and the root layout doesn't apply the `.dark` class to the HTML element, so changing the theme cookie doesn't actually change what the user sees. There's also no theme toggle UI in the sidebar yet, and I need to clarify whether the user wants me to test the current broken state or build out the missing pieces first and then test them.

I'm also trying to figure out what kind of testing they're looking for—are we doing unit tests with Vitest, end-to-end tests with Playwright, visual regression testing, or accessibility audits with tools like axe-core? Let me narrow down the most pressing questions first — whether we're testing an already-implemented theme system or building the wiring alongside testing, and what type of testing makes sense (e2e, unit, accessibility audits, or manual). Then I'll circle back on the specifics like contrast standards, which accessibility checks matter most, and how many pages we need to cover.

questions.prompt: What's the actual scope? My read is that dark mode doesn't visibly work in org-next today (cookie persists, but nothing flips a `dark` class or honors `system`). Do you want the plan to:

options.label: Test-only: assume theming works, just write tests (I'll flag any bugs they catch but not fix wiring)
Wire + test: include the minimal wiring to actually apply theme (root html `dark` class, system preference, no-flash SSR) AND add the test coverage
Audit first: produce a gap analysis + test plan as a doc, no code changes yet, decide later

questions.prompt: Which test layers do you want covered? (pick all that apply)

options.label: Playwright e2e (toggle works, persists across reload, SSR cookie -> rendered theme, system preference via emulateMedia)
Automated a11y scans (axe-core via @axe-core/playwright) on key routes in both modes
Color contrast checks on the design tokens themselves (WCAG AA pairs in theme.css)
Visual regression screenshots per mode (Playwright toHaveScreenshot)
Vitest unit tests for the theme hook / cookie validation
Documented manual QA checklist (focus rings, reduced motion, keyboard nav, screen reader labels)

content.text: Since the user didn't specify exactly what they need, I'm going to make reasonable assumptions based on their request for accessibility and functionality. I'll recommend the most comprehensive approach—wiring plus testing—and layer in multiple validation methods including end-to-end tests with Playwright, accessibility checks with axe, contrast verification, visual regression testing, and a manual checklist to ensure everything actually works.

Since you skipped the clarifications, I'll draft a plan that covers the most likely interpretation ("make dark/light theming actually work end-to-end and prove it with tests + a11y"). I'll explicitly call out the assumptions so you can redirect me if any are wrong.

 I can see the user wants a working dark/light theme with proper accessibility, so I'm putting together a plan that addresses both the missing wiring and testing coverage. The main fix is straightforward—add a small client effect to the root layout that reads the theme cookie and applies the `dark` class accordingly, then layer in comprehensive tests to verify the theming actually works across different scenarios.

input.name: theme-and-a11y-tests
input.overview: Wire org-next's persisted theme cookie up to the actual UI (the `.dark` class is never applied today), expose a theme toggle in the sidebar, and prove dark/light/system + accessibility with layered tests (Playwright e2e, axe-core, token contrast, manual checklist).
input.plan:
## Critical finding before planning org-next persists `theme: "light" | "dark" | "system"` in a signed cookie via [src/server/user-session.server.ts](apps/org-next/src/server/user-session.server.ts) and exposes it through [src/hooks/use-user-session.ts](apps/org-next/src/hooks/use-user-session.ts), but **nothing in the app applies the `.dark` class to `` or honors `system`**: - `[src/routes/__root.tsx](apps/org-next/src/routes/__root.tsx)` renders `` with no `className` derived from theme. - No `ThemeProvider` / `next-themes` import in `apps/org-next` (grep is empty). - `[src/styles.css](apps/org-next/src/styles.css)` declares `@custom-variant dark (&:is(.dark *));`, so the entire dark token set in `[packages/ui/src/styles/theme.css](packages/ui/src/styles/theme.css)` is dormant. - `[src/styles.css](apps/org-next/src/styles.css)` line 72 hardcodes `a { color: oklch(42.4% 0.199 265.638); }` — won't change in dark mode (a real a11y bug). - The sidebar in [src/routes/_authed/-app-sidebar.tsx](apps/org-next/src/routes/_authed/-app-sidebar.tsx) has no theme toggle. Conclusion: testing without first wiring will only confirm the bug. The plan therefore has a small implementation slice followed by layered verification. ## Assumptions (call these out if wrong) - "Theming" in this request means light / dark / system, not per-tenant brand theming. - We target **WCAG 2.1 AA** for contrast and standard a11y (keyboard, focus, ARIA). - We use the SSR cookie as the source of truth (avoid `next-themes`, since org-next is TanStack Start, not Next). - e2e scope: representative routes — `/login`, `/`, `/organizations/new`, `/campaigns` — both modes. ## Architecture: how theme actually gets applied ```mermaid flowchart LR cookie["userSession cookie
theme: light|dark|system"] --> rootLoader["__root loader
getUserSession()"] rootLoader --> shell["RootDocument
html className"] shell --> domLight["html class=""
:root tokens"] shell --> domDark["html class="dark"
.dark tokens"] systemPref["matchMedia
prefers-color-scheme"] -.->|"theme=system"| effect["client effect
resolves to light|dark"] effect --> domLight effect --> domDark toggle["Sidebar ThemeToggle"] -->|"setTheme()"| serverFn["updateUserSession server fn"] serverFn --> cookie ``` ## Implementation slice (minimum viable wiring) 1. **Resolve theme in `__root` shell** — `[src/routes/__root.tsx](apps/org-next/src/routes/__root.tsx)`: - Read `userSession.theme` from `useLoaderData()`. - On SSR: if `theme === "dark"` set ``. For `system`, render `` (no class) and rely on the client effect — accept a brief flash for `system` users (document this trade-off) OR send a `Sec-CH-Prefers-Color-Scheme`-aware default if we want zero flash later. - Add a small client `useEffect` that, when `theme === "system"`, listens to `window.matchMedia("(prefers-color-scheme: dark)")` and toggles `document.documentElement.classList.toggle("dark", ...)`. - Set `` so native form controls match. 2. **Theme toggle in sidebar footer** — extend [src/routes/_authed/-app-sidebar.tsx](apps/org-next/src/routes/_authed/-app-sidebar.tsx) with a small inline `ThemeToggle` (sun / moon / monitor) calling `useUserSession().setTheme(...)`. Don't import `packages/ui`'s `next-themes`-based `ThemeToggle` (`[packages/ui/src/components/theme-toggle.tsx](packages/ui/src/components/theme-toggle.tsx)`) — it would fight the cookie source of truth. Use `aria-label` + `aria-pressed`, kebab-case file `-theme-toggle.tsx`. 3. **Fix the hardcoded link color** in `[src/styles.css](apps/org-next/src/styles.css)`: replace the raw `oklch(...)` with a token-based color (e.g. `text-secondary-700` equivalent / a new `--link` pair in `theme.css`) so links are legible in dark mode. ## Verification layers ### Playwright e2e — `tests/theme.spec.ts` Use the existing fixture in `[tests/test-fixture.ts](apps/org-next/tests/test-fixture.ts)` (it already waits for `__HYDRATED__` and forwards RMP). - **Light persisted via cookie**: set the `userSession` cookie via `context.addCookies` to `theme: "light"`, navigate, assert `html.className` does **not** contain `dark` and `getComputedStyle(body).backgroundColor` matches the light `--background` token. - **Dark persisted via cookie**: same, with `theme: "dark"`, assert `html` has class `dark`. - **System honored**: set `theme: "system"`, use `page.emulateMedia({ colorScheme: "dark" })` and assert `html.dark` is present; flip to `"light"` and assert it's removed (no full reload). - **Toggle persists across reload**: from `/`, click the moon button, assert `html.dark`, reload, assert still dark (cookie round-trip). - **No FOUC for explicit choice**: navigate cold and assert the `dark` class is set on the **first** rendered HTML (use `page.content()` before hydration via a route that 200s without React; or assert `html` class matches before `__HYDRATED__` resolves by reading `await page.evaluate` in a `'domcontentloaded'` waitFor). ### Automated a11y — axe-core via `@axe-core/playwright` Add dev dep `@axe-core/playwright`, plus a small helper `tests/axe-helpers.ts`: ```ts const results = await new AxeBuilder({ page }) .withTags(["wcag2a", "wcag2aa", "wcag21aa"]) .analyze(); expect(results.violations).toEqual([]); ``` Run it across the matrix `{ /login, /, /organizations/new, /campaigns } × { light, dark }`. Use `mockServerRequest` for routes behind `_authed` (mirrors `[tests/app-sidebar.spec.ts](apps/org-next/tests/app-sidebar.spec.ts)`). ### Token contrast — `tests/theme-contrast.spec.ts` (Vitest) Pure-data check on `[packages/ui/src/styles/theme.css](packages/ui/src/styles/theme.css)`: - Parse the `:root` and `.dark` blocks; for each foreground/background pair (`background`/`foreground`, `card`/`card-foreground`, `popover`/`popover-foreground`, `primary`/`primary-foreground`, `secondary`/`secondary-foreground`, `muted`/`muted-foreground`, `accent`/`accent-foreground`, `destructive`/`destructive-foreground`, `success`/`success-foreground`, `warning`/`warning-foreground`, `sidebar-background`/`sidebar-foreground`, `sidebar-accent`/`sidebar-accent-foreground`), assert WCAG AA contrast (>= 4.5:1 normal text, >= 3:1 large/UI). Use `culori` or `colorjs.io` to compute relative luminance from oklch. Decision needed if any pair fails: tweak the token (one-line oklch nudge) or accept and add an explicit allowlist with justification. ### Manual a11y checklist — `[apps/org-next/docs/theming.md](apps/org-next/docs/theming.md)` (new doc) Items the automated layer can't reliably catch: - Keyboard: tab order through toggle; `Enter` / `Space` activate. - Focus visibility: `:focus-visible` ring contrast in both modes (especially the muted dark `--ring`). - Screen reader: announces "Light", "Dark", "System" with `aria-pressed` state changes. - `prefers-reduced-motion`: theme transition (if any) is suppressed. - Form controls / scrollbars adopt the right scheme via `color-scheme` (already in `theme.css`). - Print preview is legible. ### Visual regression (optional but cheap) Two `toHaveScreenshot()` snapshots — sidebar in light and dark — to catch drift if anyone tweaks tokens. Keep to one component, not full pages, to avoid flake. ## Doc updates (don't skip) - `[apps/org-next/docs/README.md](apps/org-next/docs/README.md)`: add a short "Theming" section linking to: - `__root.tsx` (where the `dark` class is applied) - the new `-theme-toggle.tsx` - the cookie shape (already documented) - the new `tests/theme.spec.ts` and `theme-contrast.spec.ts` - New `[apps/org-next/docs/theming.md](apps/org-next/docs/theming.md)`: SSR/system-preference trade-off, manual a11y checklist, how to add a new token pair (must include AA contrast in both modes). ## Validation order 1. `pnpm --filter org-next typecheck` 2. `pnpm --filter org-next test` (contrast unit test) 3. `pnpm --filter org-next e2e -- theme.spec.ts a11y.spec.ts` 4. `pnpm run validate` at the repo root before opening the PR (per `.cursor/rules/toolchain.mdc`). ## Out of scope (flag now if any of these belong in scope) - Per-tenant brand theming. - High-contrast mode or custom user palettes. - Dark-mode handling for Storybook (different concern in `apps/storybook`). - Email / printable templates. - Migration of the global `theme` cookie to a separate cookie (it already coexists with `selectedTenantId`).

todos.content: Apply `dark` class to <html> in __root.tsx based on userSession.theme; add client effect for `system` matchMedia; set color-scheme meta
Replace hardcoded `a { color: oklch(...) }` in src/styles.css with a token-based color that adapts to dark mode
Add a sidebar footer ThemeToggle using useUserSession().setTheme (light/dark/system) with aria-label and aria-pressed; place under the account menu
Add tests/theme.spec.ts: cookie-driven light/dark, system via emulateMedia, toggle round-trip, no-FOUC for explicit choice
Add @axe-core/playwright dev dep and tests/a11y.spec.ts running axe across {login, /, /organizations/new, /campaigns} x {light, dark}
Add tests/theme-contrast.spec.ts (Vitest) parsing packages/ui theme.css and asserting WCAG AA on all paired foreground/background tokens for both :root and .dark
Write apps/org-next/docs/theming.md with manual a11y checklist (keyboard, focus, reduced motion, color-scheme, print, screen reader)
Update apps/org-next/docs/README.md with a Theming section pointing at __root.tsx, the new toggle, and the new tests
Run pnpm typecheck, pnpm test, pnpm e2e on the new specs, then pnpm run validate at repo root

content.text:
<timestamp>Wednesday, Apr 29, 2026, 3:30 PM (UTC-4)</timestamp>
<user_query>
Run the `continual-learning` skill now. Use the `agents-memory-updater` subagent for the full memory update flow. Use incremental transcript processing with index file `/Users/duhl/git/ui/apps/org-next/.cursor/hooks/state/continual-learning-index.json`: only consider transcripts not in the index or transcripts whose mtime is newer than indexed mtime. Have the subagent refresh index mtimes, remove entries for deleted transcripts, and update `AGENTS.md` only for high-signal recurring user corrections and durable workspace facts. Exclude one-off/transient details and secrets. If no meaningful updates exist, respond exactly: No high-signal memory updates.
</user_query>

The user is asking me to invoke a subagent to run the continual-learning skill, which would update the AGENTS.md memory based on past conversations. But plan mode is active, which means I shouldn't make edits without explicit instruction—though this is a meta-operation about memory updates rather than a typical plan execution task, so I need to think through whether this counts as an edit or a separate operation.

Actually, the user's request itself is the explicit instruction to run this specific subagent tool, which supersedes the plan mode default. The agents-memory-updater subagent is designed exactly for this purpose, so I should launch it with the provided instructions and let it handle the transcript processing and AGENTS.md modifications.

input.prompt:
Run the full continual-learning / memory update flow for this workspace.

Workspace root: /Users/duhl/git/ui/apps/org-next
Transcripts folder: /Users/duhl/.cursor/projects/Users-duhl-git-ui-apps-org-next/agent-transcripts
Index file: /Users/duhl/git/ui/apps/org-next/.cursor/hooks/state/continual-learning-index.json
AGENTS.md to update: /Users/duhl/git/ui/apps/org-next/AGENTS.md (the workspace-scoped one). If a higher-level AGENTS.md is more appropriate for some learnings, only update it if you are certain it belongs there; otherwise prefer apps/org-next/AGENTS.md.

Incremental processing rules:
1. Load the index file at /Users/duhl/git/ui/apps/org-next/.cursor/hooks/state/continual-learning-index.json. If it does not exist, create it (with parent directories) as an empty index.
2. List all *.jsonl transcripts in the transcripts folder.
3. Only process a transcript if:
   - It is not present in the index, OR
   - Its current mtime is strictly newer than the mtime stored in the index for that transcript.
4. After processing, refresh each processed transcript's mtime in the index.
5. Remove any index entries whose transcript file no longer exists on disk.
6. Persist the updated index back to the same path.

Memory update rules for AGENTS.md:
- Only add high-signal, recurring user corrections and durable workspace facts that future agents will need.
- Specifically look for patterns like: "Learned User Preferences", "Learned Workspace Facts", repeated stylistic corrections, repeated architectural rules, repeated test-infra rules, repeated do-not-do directives.
- Exclude one-off/transient details, ephemeral debugging notes, session-specific decisions, and anything that looks like secrets, tokens, internal URLs with credentials, or PII.
- Do not duplicate existing entries; merge/dedupe with the current AGENTS.md content. Keep the file concise and the existing section structure intact.
- Prefer concrete, actionable phrasing matching the existing style in apps/org-next/AGENTS.md.

Output rules:
- If you make updates, briefly summarize (in 1-3 sentences) what categories of facts/preferences you added or merged, and confirm the index was updated.
- If after processing all eligible transcripts there are NO meaningful updates, respond with EXACTLY this string and nothing else:
  No high-signal memory updates.
- Do not invent learnings; if signal is weak, prefer the "no updates" response.

Constraints:
- You may read transcripts, the index file, and AGENTS.md files. You may write to the index file and AGENTS.md only.
- Do not modify any other files.
- Do not run validation (no pnpm/test/build/etc.).

content.text:
Memory update complete. Two durable items merged into [apps/org-next/AGENTS.md](apps/org-next/AGENTS.md):

- Learned User Preference: default route error UI must keep stacks / raw JSON dumps out of the prominent body (relegate to a "technical details" panel).
- Learned Workspace Fact: Playwright e2e loads env from `apps/org-next/.env.e2e` (not `.env.test`); SSR fetch mocking via RMP requires `VITE_ENABLE_RMP=true` or server functions bypass mocks and hit the real org API.

Index at [.cursor/hooks/state/continual-learning-index.json](apps/org-next/.cursor/hooks/state/continual-learning-index.json) now has 80 entries with mtimes refreshed and no orphans.

Plan mode is still active and the theming-and-a11y plan is unchanged. Let me know when you want me to start executing it (or if you want to refine it first).
